title: Rich Fermented Kenyan Beans Stew
description: A dense, indulgent stew of fermented black beans, sweet potato and soy lardons, finished with a splash of colatura di alici and aromatic spices, all cooked in a wok for a single-pan delight.
instructions: Heat the wok over medium‑high and pour in the vegetable oil, letting it shimmer before adding the soy lardons.
Fry the lardons until golden and crispy, then set them aside on a paper towel.
Add the minced garlic to the wok, sautéing until fragrant, about 30 seconds.
Stir in the tomato paste, smoked paprika, cumin, and thyme, cooking the spices for 1 minute to bloom their flavors.
Pour in the colatura di alici and a splash of water, then bring the mixture to a simmer.
Add the diced sweet potato, diced tomato, and fermented black beans to the wok, tossing to coat them in the sauce.
Reduce the heat to medium, cover the wok, and let the stew simmer for 25 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Once the sweet potato is tender, fold in the crispy soy lardons and season with black pepper and salt.
Continue to simmer uncovered for an additional 5 minutes to thicken the stew.
Remove the wok from heat and transfer the stew to a serving bowl.
Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro if desired, and serve hot.
